question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $68,000 on April 14?

fragility_points: Crypto downside volatility can be abrupt.
key_assumptions: Binance BTC/USDT trading and the settlement-relevant candle feed remain operational through the resolution window.
No sudden shock causes a greater-than-6% downside move into the settlement minute.
The Binance API and UI candle used for resolution remain aligned in the ordinary way.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket rules make Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close the governing source of truth.
Current Binance spot is materially above 68000 with less than 24 hours to resolution.
Outside-view recent-history base rates favor remaining above 68000 from such starting levels.
Single-minute and single-venue settlement narrowness prevents treating the outcome as certain.
Net result is a high-probability Yes estimate slightly below the market.
payload.main_thesis: BTC/USDT on Binance is likely to stay above 68000 for the April 14 12:00 ET settlement minute because spot is already more than 6% above the threshold with less than 24 hours left, though single-minute settlement fragility keeps this below certainty.
quote_anchors: The close must be strictly greater than 68,000.
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality direct Binance data plus explicit Polymarket rules gave a solid basis; evidence independence was medium and source-of-truth ambiguity was low to medium because settlement is narrow but clearly specified.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact Binance 1-minute close at 12:00 ET, so a sharp intraday selloff or exchange-specific issue could still flip the result.
Exactly 68000 resolves No because the close must be strictly higher than the threshold.
strongest_supports: Binance direct price check during the run showed BTC around 72.16k-72.19k, about 6.1% above the threshold.
Binance 24h low was still near 70.5k, above 68k.
Recent Binance hourly history showed that when BTC was already above 68k it remained above 68k 24 hours later about 88.9% of the time.
payload.timing_relevance: Resolution is on 2026-04-14 at 12:00 ET, which maps to 16:00 UTC; with less than 24 hours left, spot-to-threshold distance is the main base-rate input.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether Binance UI settlement surface could diverge operationally from the API despite ordinary alignment.
How much tail risk should be assigned to exchange-specific disruption before noon ET.
payload.what_would_change_view: A drop toward 68.5k-69k, exchange-specific Binance issues, a major macro or crypto shock, or a clarified alternative reading of settlement timing would lower the estimate.

strongest_disconfirmers: Bitcoin can move 5-6% in less than a day, and this contract settles on a single one-minute exchange-specific close.
